key_themes: Profit growth and margin expansion from strategic initiatives
Strong brand performance (record Vimto sales) and UK market share gains
Analyst upgrades with substantial price target upside (~45%)
International expansion success (concentrate model in Africa) and operational efficiencies (ERP implementation)
Dividend growth and focus on shareholder returns
summary: Nichols plc (NICL.L), the UK soft drinks group behind the Vimto brand, reported strong 2025 preliminary results in March 2026, with group revenue up 1.3% to £175.1m, adjusted operating profit rising 9.9% to £31.7m, and margins expanding to 19.2% thanks to strategic execution, a shift to a higher-margin concentrate model internationally, record UK sales, and efficiencies including a new ERP system.[[1]](https://www.londonstockexchange.com/news-article/NICL/2025-preliminary-results/17497087)[[2]](https://x.com/tradingfloorai/status/2031632334319923472) Analysts maintain largely bullish views with an average 12-month price target of approximately 1,370-1,390p (implying ~45% upside from the recent ~950p level), supported by 'Strong Buy'/'Buy' ratings and notes on the de-rating having gone too far.[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NICL.L/)[[4]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/NICL/forecast/) While social media and investor discussion is relatively light (typical for this AIM-listed consumer staples name), reaction to the results and trading updates has been positive, focusing on profit delivery, dividend increases, positive 2026 trading, and long-term growth potential despite modest revenue growth and prior periods of share price underperformance.
sources: 5+ news/articles on Yahoo Finance, LSE RNS, Investing.com, MarketBeat (earnings release March 2026, analyst targets), ~8-10 relevant X posts (primarily earnings announcements and summaries from March 2026)
